title: Sour Crunchy Australian Pork Back Ribs Stir-fry
description: This dish is a post-workout meal featuring tender pork back ribs cooked with a tangy sauce for added flavor and crunch.
instructions: Pat dry the pork with paper towels.
Season both sides of the ribs with kosher salt and black pepper.
Heat a wok or large skillet over high heat. Add olive oil, swirl to coat the pan.
Add garlic paste and stir-fry for about 30 seconds until fragrant but not browned.
Pour in soy sauce, mirin, honey, vinegar, red pepper flakes, and cornstarch water mixture (1 tablespoon water with cornstarch stirred into it). Stir to combine all ingredients.
Place the ribs in the pan and cook for 8-10 minutes on each side until browned and caramelized. The sauce should cling to the ribs.
Reduce heat to medium-high, pour remaining soy sauce over ribs while cooking, and continue stirring until thickened slightly.
Remove from heat and serve hot with steamed vegetables.
